implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: Add PoisonEpisodeError exception class for non-recoverable failures
Add TransientIngestError exception class for recoverable failures
Document default-to-transient policy in module docstring
Ensure exceptions are distinguishable by type
Verify exceptions are not in the same inheritance subtree
Follow existing error class patterns in fleet_memory.errors

completion_promises.criterion_text: PoisonEpisodeError and TransientIngestError subclass the existing fleet-memory error base
completion_promises.evidence: Both PoisonEpisodeError and TransientIngestError now subclass Exception, consistent with existing error classes in fleet_memory.errors module. Verified by test_poison_episode_error_subclasses_exception and test_transient_ingest_error_subclasses_exception.

completion_promises.criterion_text: PoisonEpisodeError carries a reason string suitable for recording on the dead-letter subject
completion_promises.evidence: PoisonEpisodeError.__init__ accepts a required 'reason' parameter stored as self.reason attribute, accessible for DLQ recording. Verified by test_poison_episode_error_carries_reason.

completion_promises.criterion_text: The two classes are distinguishable by type (not by message string)
completion_promises.evidence: PoisonEpisodeError and TransientIngestError are separate class definitions with no inheritance relationship between them. Type-based exception handling can distinguish them reliably without string parsing. Verified by test_exceptions_are_not_in_same_subtree.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Module docstring states the default-to-transient policy for unenumerated exceptions
completion_promises.evidence: Updated module docstring in errors.py to include comprehensive Exception Taxonomy section documenting the default-to-transient policy: 'Any *unenumerated* exception escaping the service layer is treated as transient (nak + redeliver), never as poison. Losing data is worse than redelivering.' Verified by test_module_docstring_states_default_to_transient_policy.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit test asserts the two are not in the same subtree such that a TransientIngestError could be caught as poison (or vice versa)
completion_promises.evidence: Created test_exceptions_are_not_in_same_subtree which verifies: (1) neither is a subclass of the other using issubclass(), (2) attempting to catch PoisonEpisodeError as TransientIngestError fails, (3) attempting to catch TransientIngestError as PoisonEpisodeError fails. This ensures no accidental cross-catching.
